title: polypodiopsida
intro: The rank Polypodiopsida is a division of plants that includes the ferns and their allies. It is a group of vascular plants characterized by the presence of true leaves and a distinctive life cycle involving two generations of plants, known as sporophytes and gametophytes. The Polypodiopsida division encompasses a wide range of fern species, from the small, delicate maidenhair ferns to the large, tree-like horsetails. Other divisions of plants that belong to different ranks include the Commelinidae (monocots with three-veined leaves), the Rosidae (dicots with a rose-like flower structure), and the Asteridae (dicots with a daisy-like flower structure).

subclasses.subclass_desc: Oleosane is a subclass of flowering plants within the class Magnoliopsida.
Polypodiidae is a subclass of ferns that includes about 6,000 species of ferns, divided into two orders: Osmundales and Polypodiales.
Oleosanginae is a subtribe of flowering plants in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family.
Poliopodiidae is a subfamily of ferns, specifically the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the maidenhair ferns.
